1 Three or more credits required from these previously offered courses, or other relevant courses in Jewish literature, Israeli ﬁlm, Biblical Hebrew, etc. may be approved by the advisor.

Note: it may be possible to arrange with JWST and ISRL professors to provide course texts and accept assignments in Hebrew rather than English. The language of instruction is often related to the proﬁciency abilities of the students in the class.

Note: Students who place into the advanced level may take HEBR313, HEBR314, and HEBR249. They must replace HEBR207 (a required course at the intermediate level) with the equivalent number of credits, selected in consultation with, or with the approval of, the Advisor.
